title: Andrei Speriatu

infobox.value: (born 29 September 1957) is a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") former professional [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") who played as a [goalkeeper](/wiki/Goalkeeper_(association_football) "Goalkeeper (association football)"). Speriatu is considered a legend of [Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești") and [Sportul Studențesc](/wiki/FC_Sportul_Studen%C8%9Besc_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Sportul Studențesc București"), also playing for teams such as [Dinamo București](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București"), [Shimshon Tel Aviv](/wiki/Shimshon_Tel_Aviv_F.C. "Shimshon Tel Aviv F.C.") or [Dacia Pitești](/wiki/FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Dacia Pitești").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-prosport-2)

first_paragraph: **Andrei Speriatu** (born 29 September 1957) is a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") former professional [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") who played as a [goalkeeper](/wiki/Goalkeeper_(association_football) "Goalkeeper (association football)"). Speriatu is considered a legend of [Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești") and [Sportul Studențesc](/wiki/FC_Sportul_Studen%C8%9Besc_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Sportul Studențesc București"), also playing for teams such as [Dinamo București](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București"), [Shimshon Tel Aviv](/wiki/Shimshon_Tel_Aviv_F.C. **Andrei Speriatu** (born 29 September 1957) is a [Romanian](/wiki/Romania "Romania") former professional [footballer](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") who played as a [goalkeeper](/wiki/Goalkeeper_(association_football) "Goalkeeper (association football)"). Speriatu is considered a legend of [Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești") and [Sportul Studențesc](/wiki/FC_Sportul_Studen%C8%9Besc_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Sportul Studențesc București"), also playing for teams such as [Dinamo București](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București"), [Shimshon Tel Aviv](/wiki/Shimshon_Tel_Aviv_F.C. "Shimshon Tel Aviv F.C.") or [Dacia Pitești](/wiki/FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Dacia Pitești").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-prosport-2)

## Club career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrei_Speriatu&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Club career")]

Speriatu was born on 29 September 1957 in [Toporu](/wiki/Toporu "Toporu"), Romania and began playing junior-level football in 1972 at local club Spicu.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-prosport-2) In 1973 he joined [Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești") where he made his [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I") debut on 21 November 1976 when coach [Florin Halagian](/wiki/Florin_Halagian "Florin Halagian") sent him in the 63rd minute to replace [Cristian Gheorghe](/wiki/Cristian_Gheorghe "Cristian Gheorghe") in a 3–1 away loss to [UTA Arad](/wiki/FC_UTA_Arad "FC UTA Arad").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-prosport-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) In the [1978–79 UEFA Cup](/wiki/1978%E2%80%9379_UEFA_Cup "1978–79 UEFA Cup") edition he helped the team eliminate [Panathinaikos](/wiki/Panathinaikos_F.C. "Panathinaikos F.C.") in the first round with a 5–1 aggregate victory.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[4]](#cite_note-WF-4) In the following round they met [Valencia](/wiki/Valencia_CF "Valencia CF") led by [Mario Kempes](/wiki/Mario_Kempes "Mario Kempes"), earning a 2–1 win in the first leg, but they lost the second one with 5–2, thus the campaign ended.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[4]](#cite_note-WF-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) In the [same season](/wiki/1978%E2%80%9379_Divizia_A "1978–79 Divizia A") he helped Argeș win the title, being used by coach Halagian in 17 games, having to compete to be first-choice goalkeeper with Cristian Gheorghe.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

In 1980, Speriatu went to play for one season at [Dinamo București](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București"), working with coach [Valentin Stănescu](/wiki/Valentin_St%C4%83nescu "Valentin Stănescu") who used him regularly as they earned a runner-up position in the [league](/wiki/1980%E2%80%9381_Divizia_A "1980–81 Divizia A").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-prosport-2)[[7]](#cite_note-7) Afterwards he and [Constantin Pană](/wiki/Constantin_Pan%C4%83 "Constantin Pană") were transferred from Dinamo to [Sportul Studențesc București](/wiki/FC_Sportul_Studen%C8%9Besc_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Sportul Studențesc București") in exchange for [Dumitru Moraru](/wiki/Dumitru_Moraru "Dumitru Moraru").[[8]](#cite_note-8) There, he spent five seasons, the highlight of this period being a second place in the [1985–86](/wiki/1985%E2%80%9386_Divizia_A "1985–86 Divizia A") season.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-prosport-2) He also kept clean sheet in a 1–0 victory against [Inter Milan](/wiki/Inter_Milan "Inter Milan") in the [1984–85 UEFA Cup](/wiki/1984%E2%80%9385_UEFA_Cup "1984–85 UEFA Cup"), but the second leg was lost with 2–0.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[9]](#cite_note-9)

In 1986, Speriatu returned to Argeș Pitești for four seasons, then made a comeback for one year at Sportul Studențesc.[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-prosport-2) In 1991 he played abroad for Israeli [second league](/wiki/Liga_Leumit "Liga Leumit") side [Shimshon Tel Aviv](/wiki/Shimshon_Tel_Aviv_F.C. "Shimshon Tel Aviv F.C.").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-prosport-2) Afterwards he came back for a third spell at Sportul Studențesc where he made his last Divizia A appearance on 20 June 1993 in a 4–2 away loss to Dinamo, totaling 406 appearances in the competition and 10 games in the [UEFA Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Europa_League "UEFA Europa League").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[2]](#cite_note-prosport-2) Speriatu ended his career in 1994, after he spent one season at [Dacia Pitești](/wiki/Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "Dacia Pitești") in [Divizia C](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III"), helping it gain promotion to the [second league](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II").[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)[[10]](#cite_note-GSP-10)

## International career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrei_Speriatu&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: International career")]

Speriatu made four appearances for [Romania](/wiki/Romania_national_football_team "Romania national football team"), making his debut on 13 May 1979 under coach [Florin Halagian](/wiki/Florin_Halagian "Florin Halagian") in a 1–1 draw against [Cyprus](/wiki/Cyprus_national_football_team "Cyprus national football team") in the [Euro 1980 qualifiers](/wiki/UEFA_Euro_1980_qualifying_Group_3 "UEFA Euro 1980 qualifying Group 3").[[11]](#cite_note-EU-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) His following three matches were friendlies, the last one taking place on 17 March 1986 when he kept a clean sheet in a 0–0 draw against [Iraq](/wiki/Iraq_national_football_team "Iraq national football team").[[11]](#cite_note-EU-11)

### International stats

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrei_Speriatu&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: International stats")]

| --- | --- | --- |

| 1979 | 1 | 0 |
| 1980 | 1 | 0 |
| 1986 | 2 | 0 |
| Total | 4 | 0 |

## Coaching career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrei_Speriatu&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Coaching career")]

Speriatu had his first coaching experience at [Dacia Pitești](/wiki/FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Dacia Pitești"), then from 1997 until 1998 he was head coach of [Sportul Studențesc](/wiki/FC_Sportul_Studen%C8%9Besc_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Sportul Studențesc București").[[2]](#cite_note-prosport-2)[[10]](#cite_note-GSP-10)[[13]](#cite_note-jurnalul-13) Afterwards he worked as a goalkeeper coach for several clubs and as an assistant on several occasions for [Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești") under head coaches [Nicolae Dobrin](/wiki/Nicolae_Dobrin "Nicolae Dobrin"), [Constantin Stancu](/wiki/Constantin_Stancu "Constantin Stancu"), [Marian Bondrea](/wiki/Marian_Bondrea "Marian Bondrea") and [Bogdan Vintilă](/wiki/Bogdan_Vintil%C4%83 "Bogdan Vintilă").[[2]](#cite_note-prosport-2)[[10]](#cite_note-GSP-10)[[13]](#cite_note-jurnalul-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14) He also worked at Argeș's Center for Children and Juniors and was a scouter for the same team.[[2]](#cite_note-prosport-2)[[10]](#cite_note-GSP-10)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Andrei_Speriatu&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Honours")]

**Argeș Pitești**

* [Divizia A](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"): [1978–79](/wiki/1978%E2%80%9379_Divizia_A "1978–79 Divizia A")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)

**Dacia Pitești**

* [Divizia C](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III"): [1993–94](/wiki/1993%E2%80%9394_Divizia_C "1993–94 Divizia C")[[1]](#cite_note-RS-1)
